Co-authored-by: DCP Paperclip Agent <agents@dcp.sa>
Co-authored-by: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(ksu,ci): harden module hook execution and workflow validation

Align the module install and boot hooks with KernelSU's actual execution
model and add CI safeguards that catch the same class of packaging and
runtime regressions before release.

The main fix is in `customize.sh`. KernelSU installers may source the
customization hook through `sh`, which makes `$0` unreliable and means the
previous self-reexec path could resolve the wrong module directory during
installation. Replace that behavior with explicit module root discovery that
prefers `MODPATH`, respects `DUCK_TOOLBOX_ROOT`, and falls back to stable
`/data/adb/modules_update/duck-toolbox` and `/data/adb/modules/duck-toolbox`
locations. This ensures the install hook repairs the real staged module
directory instead of silently operating on an incorrect path when the module
is unpacked into `modules_update`.

While touching the installer hook, prefer KernelSU installer helpers when
they are available. Use `set_perm` and `set_perm_recursive` to repair the
backend executable bits and the private runtime tree during installation, and
retain the existing `chmod`/`find` fallback path for non-installer contexts.
This keeps runtime permissions correct both inside the official installer and
when the script is exercised manually in simpler shell environments.

Apply the same module-root hardening to `service.sh` so the boot-time repair
path no longer depends on a brittle `${0%/*}` assumption. The service hook
now resolves the real module directory from explicit environment variables
and known module locations before it attempts to repair `duckctl.sh`,
`duckd`, and the `var/` runtime hierarchy. Together with the install-hook
changes, this closes the gap where the archive could be unpacked
successfully but still fail to initialize its runtime state correctly.

Add a repository-level `.gitattributes` file to force LF line endings for
module shell scripts, workflow YAML, and `module.prop`. This is especially
important for Windows-based development, where CRLF conversion can break
KernelSU module metadata parsing or shell execution in subtle ways even when
the archive structure itself looks correct.

Strengthen `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so these failure modes are caught
automatically. Add a line-ending verification step that fails the build if
`module.prop`, `customize.sh`, `service.sh`, or `bin/duckctl.sh` contains
CRLF. Add a dedicated KernelSU module-hook smoke test that stages the module,
removes executable bits from `duckctl.sh` and `duckd`, sources
`customize.sh`, executes `service.sh`, and asserts that the runtime
directories are recreated and the backend files are restored to executable
state. These checks make the workflow validate the install semantics rather
than only the final ZIP layout.

Also clean up the Telegram delivery workflow details while auditing CI:
switch the job environment name to `Telegram Env` so it matches the expected
secret scope, and restrict artifact discovery to `*.zip` files so the upload
step cannot accidentally pick an unrelated download output.

Overall, this change turns the recent install failure investigation into a
concrete fix set: it aligns the module hooks with KernelSU expectations,
prevents Windows line-ending drift from corrupting release artifacts, and
teaches CI to verify the install-time and boot-time behaviors that matter for
real devices.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Self-contain Docker workflow and seed state (#74)

* feat(docker): self-contain workflow and seed state

Summary:
- move Docker workflow and seed repo state into named volumes
- load repo-root WORKFLOW.md prompt and hooks for Docker workspaces
- add Docker setup, seed sync, and config import/export helpers

Rationale:
- remove steady-state host bind dependencies from Docker mode
- let workspace behavior come from the configured repo with a safe fallback
- keep deployment config separate from repo-local prompt and hook behavior

commit.message:
refactor(tests): remove dead_code allowances (#10)

## Why
- Reduce `dead_code` suppression noise in the test tree.
- Keep each integration test target compiling only the helpers it
actually uses.

## What
- Split the shared `tests/support/mod.rs` helper into smaller support
modules grouped by use case.
- Update each integration test file to import only the support it needs.
- Replace the test-only `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper with direct
`MeasureOptions` struct construction.

## Testing
- `cargo check --tests`
- `cargo test anafanafo::tests::anafanafo_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test badge_maker_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test raw_css_variable_input_falls_back_like_badge_maker`
- `cargo test invalid_id_suffix_matches_badge_maker_error`

## Risks
- This removes the public `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper. External
callers should construct `MeasureOptions { font, guess }` directly
instead.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- strengthen the project overview rules so new projects maintain a live
  PR review priority dashboard at the top of the Linear description

Rationale:
- template workflows should not strand merged work in `Merging` just
  because the orchestrator stopped routing that state
- the template should encode the live dashboard behavior explicitly so
  downstream projects do not drift into one-off overview prose

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- tighten project overview instructions so the Linear description stays
  a live PR review priority dashboard instead of drifting into a stale
  narrative summary

Rationale:
- StackPerf had several merged issues stranded in `Merging` because the
  workflow stopped polling them after the human state transition
- the live dashboard only helps reviewers if agents keep regenerating it
  in place as queue conditions change

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ci(coverage): upload reports to Codecov (#7)

## Why
- Make Rust test coverage visible in CI and publish it to Codecov.
- Enforce a 30 second limit per `nextest` test case to avoid hung
reference-driven tests.
- Closes #6.

## What
- Install `llvm-tools-preview` and `cargo-llvm-cov` in the existing
`check` job.
- Generate `lcov` coverage during the `nextest` run and upload
`target/llvm-cov/lcov.info` to Codecov via OIDC.
- Keep doc tests in the same CI job without adding a separate coverage
job.
- Tighten the shared `nextest` timeout to 30 seconds per case.

## Testing
- `cargo nextest list --config-file .config/nextest.toml`
- Full CI coverage run not executed locally because this machine does
not have `bun`; the workflow installs `bun` before running the
reference-backed tests.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent stale resurfacing, weak invalidation, and meta-process chatter (#37)

* fix: prevent stale resurfacing, weak invalidation, and meta-process chatter in heartbeat

Addresses the core bugs described in #33:

1. Resolved fence: decay processor now prevents StateResolved memories from
   transitioning back to active/stale. Resolved memories can only move forward
   (to archived/deleted), closing the zombie resurfacing loop caused by
   access-burst importance boosts.

2. Meta-process filter: PendingWork signals now skip internal agent bookkeeping
   content (monitoring, syncing, cleanup, etc.) so it never reaches users.

3. State-aware dedup with suppression footprint: shouldSuppressTopicRepeat now
   checks recently suppressed decisions (not just "act" decisions) with a 2x
   dedup window, catching state-transition zombies where a memory's state
   changes and the same fingerprint re-enters the pipeline.

4. New GetRecentDecisions store method returns all heartbeat decisions (not just
   "act") within a time window, powering the suppression footprint layer.

Closes #33

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(storage): cover get recent decisions

* test: cover suppression footprint dedup

* fix: tighten meta-process filtering and decay reasons

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: keyoku-engine
repository.description: AI memory engine — semantic search, knowledge graph, 12-signal heartbeat, memory decay

commit.message:
fix(cli): publish control-plane before bootstrap recovery

Summary:
- start the control-plane server with an immediate lightweight snapshot
  before scheduler bootstrap runs
- publish the recovered bootstrap snapshot after recovery completes so
  the TUI can refresh with recovered issues

Rationale:
- long or variable bootstrap recovery work could leave the TUI stuck
  awaiting its first snapshot and showing `conn=reconnecting`
- serving the control plane first keeps the TUI attachable right away
  while preserving the startup recovery behavior from the earlier fix

Tests:
- cargo check
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p opensymphony-orchestrator --test scheduler
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli orchestrator_run::backends::tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: OpenSymphony
repository.description: OpenSymphony

Session-ID: 019d23be-6872-7270-9921-bb44c983806a

fix(orchestrator): recover startup state before launch (#67)

* fix(orchestrator): speed restart recovery and worker launches

Summary:
- recover managed workspaces from `.opensymphony` metadata during
  orchestrator startup and publish the recovered snapshot before the
  first launch wave
- batch ready worker launches so reused OpenHands conversations attach
  concurrently within the existing scheduler capacity limits
- remove a redundant pre-attach event-history fetch and log launch
  failures that previously looked like silent retry loops

Rationale:
- large reused conversations were making both restart recovery and
  launch-time attach path latency much worse than necessary
- publishing bootstrap state early keeps the TUI useful during restart
  even when conversation attach still takes time
- explicit recovery metadata and launch diagnostics make stuck
  `retry_queued` issues easier to observe and debug

Tests:
- cargo fmt
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands runtime_stream_replays_initial_snapshot_events_on_attach
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli orchestrator_run::backends::tests
- cargo test -p opensymphony-orchestrator --test scheduler

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): satisfy clippy in worker launch batching

* fix(cli): log concurrent launch waiter failures

* fix(orchestrator): preserve launch waiter failure details

* fix(orchestrator): avoid repeated state normalization

* fix(orchestrator): reuse normalized state limit lookups

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime): refresh stale realtime playbooks

Summary:
- reject empty or fallback current-session playbooks during reuse
- regenerate realtime playbooks when scanner candidate sets change
- log LLM timeout and exception types for home-network openai_compat runs

Rationale:
- restarting in the same session should not pin the runtime to stale fallback
- scanner-driven realtime loops need fresh playbooks when the active universe changes
- first-time self-hosted LLM serving needs clearer timeout diagnostics

Tests:
- pytest tests/test_main.py -k "decide_playbook_selection or load_stored_playbook_for_session or reuses_stored_regular_session_playbook_after_restart or regenerates_playbook_after_restart_when_scanner_candidates_change" -v
- pytest tests/test_main.py -k "restores_pre_refresh_playbook_when_mid_session_refresh_generation_fails or regenerates_playbook_on_us_regular_session_transition or load_or_generate_daily_playbook_reuses_latest_current_session_entry or forces_rescan_on_market_session_transition_even_after_state_update" -v
- pytest tests/test_brain.py -k "playbook or malformed_json or prompt_override" -v
- ruff check src/main.py tests/test_main.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(linux): align desktop icon integration with AppImage runtime

- install the launcher only when APPIMAGE is present
- ship icon.png as a root resource and copy it to hicolor/1024x1024
- write absolute icon path plus StartupWMClass in the desktop entry
- mark the generated desktop file as trusted on GNOME-based desktops

commit.message:
fix: harden TTS message button state handling (#86)

* fix: TTS play/stop button sync with player state

Abort the fetch stream when stopping TTS so the player doesn't restart
from incoming data. Wait for playback to actually start before polling
for completion to prevent premature onEnd callbacks.

commit.message:
Fix RECOG.HomProjDet to correctly deal with scalar multiples (#411)

This function is used by the `ProjDeterminant` recognition method
to compute images, and it was not handling the case were a given
element was scaled by an element of an extension field of what it
expected.

Also refine tensor docs (text fully written by Codex).

Fixes #33
Fixes #302

commit.message:
Fix projective TrivialGroup lifting (#412)

For projective groups we use one actual input scalar as the leaf's
nice generator, instead of the identity word, so parent nodes can
lift words back through the image without losing the scalar
representative.

Fixes #392.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: harden runtime flow and modernize packaging

- migrate the app to better-sqlite3 and user-scoped data/log paths
- replace ad-hoc asset packaging with electron-builder and release workflow
- add a rotating file logger plus Vitest coverage for critical paths
- harden Electron IPC with a typed preload API and disabled nodeIntegration
- fix startup scheduling, prevent overlapping speed tests, and add speedtest timeouts
- decouple public IP enrichment from the core measurement flow with retry support
- refresh the main view/history after delayed IP resolution and improve failure UX
- escape Linux desktop Exec paths for packaged builds with spaces
- align docs and CI around Node 24 and rewrite the README in Spanish

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(tyir): add const-eval interpreter (#27)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a TyIR const-folding interpreter and integrated it into
compile/inspect/repl flows.

* **Bug Fixes / Improvements**
  * Richer const-eval diagnostics with source spans and call stacks.
* Revised string ABI to a structured string record; updated
extern/runtime calling conventions and lowering so owned/borrowed
strings are handled explicitly.
  * Preserve declaration-level `runtime` on functions for const-eval.

* **Tests**
* New and updated tests for folding, string ABI, and const-eval
errors/limits.

* **Documentation**
* Updated runtime/extern, stdlib I/O/ABI, and TyIR/runtime docs and
examples.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
